location: summary_report.md §3, CLCC1 bullet: 'among the more T-cell-specific hits (measured K562 concordance)'
issue: Contradicted by data. CLCC1 K562 concordance = 0.4769 (candidates_for_workup.csv) is the HIGHEST — i.e. LEAST T-cell-specific — of all 8 candidates with measured K562 data (rank 8/8; range 0.264 TATDN2 to 0.477 CLCC1). It passes the binary spec_call='T-cell-specific(measured)' filter but is the weakest of the measured set, so 'among the more T-cell-specific' overstates it. The detailed workup does NOT make this claim, only the summary does.
fix: Reword to 'measured (K562 concordance 0.48 — the highest/least-specific of the measured set)' or drop the specificity qualifier. Low downstream impact: CLCC1 is explicitly not carried forward.

location: summary_report.md Fig 3 caption: 'Knockdown direction is consistent across conditions.'
issue: Overstated for the panel as drawn. In Fig 3 panel C (and polar_score_by_condition.csv), CD6 flips sign Rest/Stim8h/Stim48h = +0.006/+0.030/-0.041 (visible as red->blue at 48h); INTS6 (-0.073/-0.058/+0.003) and TATDN2 (+0.038/+0.072/+0.001) decay through ~0 by 48h (INTS6 a marginal sign flip). The 12 polarization NOMINATIONS hold sign at 8h vs Rest, but the blanket caption covers all 14 rows shown, including CD6 which clearly flips.
fix: Qualify: 'Direction is consistent across conditions for the polarization nominations; the exploratory activation gene CD6 flips sign by 48 h, and INTS6/TATDN2 decay to ~0.'

location: coverage_table.csv / reports — INTS6, SNRPB2 novelty vs chembl_target_id
issue: INTS6 (chembl_target_id CHEMBL5724659) and SNRPB2 (CHEMBL5725125) are called 'genuinely-new' while carrying a registered ChEMBL target ID. This is honest, not an error: both have 0 known drugs, ~0-1 ligands, and the text qualifies 'genuinely-new (as a target)' / notes the registered-but-empty entry. Noted only so a reader doesn't read the ChEMBL ID as contradicting the novelty call.
fix: No change required; the 'as a target / no mechanisms/drugs' qualifier already present is sufficient.

issue: Row-for-row match. Novelty calls (5 recovered-known TNFRSF9/CD6/IFNAR1/CBLB/RAC2; 9 genuinely-new; PAXIP1 partial), tractability classes, genetic_support levels, direction_of_effect, and safety_call all match report tables. No tractable/druggable label without has_PDB=True + known clinical drug; no genuinely-new label on a gene with drugs or non-zero drug programs.

CBLB direction_of_effect='risk-on-LoF' correctly mapped: LoF raises autoimmune risk -> desirable for brake-release/anti-tumor, liability for autoimmune (stated). IFNAR1 & CD6 'protective-on-LoF' correctly paired with approved antagonists (anifrolumab, itolizumab). KD-lowers/raises-axis to therapeutic-direction mapping is internally consistent.

Failed validation (Spearman rho 0.13, AUROC 0.49, sign 76%) stated in summary §1 table, §3 activation header, §4 point 4, all three activation bullets (CBLB/CD6/CLCC1 flagged exploratory), and Fig 1 right-panel title ('does not clear bar'). Consistently disclosed everywhere activation hits appear.

Reconcile exactly over all 15 incl. CLCC1: tractability 4 tractable / 7 challenging / 4 intractable => 11 challenging-to-intractable; safety 2 clean / 10 watch / 3 liability => 13 watch-or-liability. Gap census also checks: 4/15 no genetic support (CLCC1,INTS6,TATDN2,URM1), 7/15 no-K562-data.

Panel titles show rho=0.72 (PASS) and rho=0.13 (does not clear bar), matching the §1 table; n=3830 matched matches text. Known regulators (red) follow the trend on the polarization panel (correct rank order) and scatter with no trend on the activation panel. Axes labelled correctly.

Nominations highlighted by direction (blue Th1 x6, red Th2 x6 = 12 polarization noms), hubs greyed (legend present), knowns labelled '(known)' (IFNAR1/RAC2/TNFRSF9), y-axis direction annotated. No crossing leader lines (perceptual). Activation genes CD6/CBLB/CLCC1 correctly absent from this polarization plot.
